zotero.abstractNote: We study the design of policies in environments where agents' welfare evolves over time in response to policy interventions. We model this as a dynamic system where a policymaker chooses a sequence of policies to maximize a long-run welfare objective. We characterize the optimal policy design problem and show that it can be framed as a constrained optimization problem. We provide conditions under which the optimal policy is stationary and discuss the implications for policy design in practice.
zotero.conferenceName: ACM Conference on Equity and Access in Algorithms, Mechanisms, and Optimization
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the Fifth ACM Conference on Equity and Access in Algorithms, Mechanisms, and Optimization
